<fa>To move to a location on the map, place the cursor<f8>over its corresponding marker and press <d1>V.<f8><f8><f8><fa>When at a city or castle, selecting its symbol and<f8>pressing <d1>V will open a menu displaying the various<f8>establishments at that location.<f8><f8><fa>If you are unsure what a particular menu option is<f8>for, press SELECT to view more information about it.<f8><f8><f8><fa>Pressing <d9><c7> while on the world map opens the main<f8>menu, from which you can access your party roster or<f8>save the game.<f8><f8><fa>The game's various tutorials can also be accessed<f8>from the main menu. These tutorials provide in-depth<f8>explanations of various aspects of the game. Consult<f8>them if you have questions about how to play the game.<f5><66><f6>A<f5>9<f6>1

<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Fantastic! You've just earned the title of<f8>Master Treasure Hunter!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It's a true ancient wonder—a remnant of the<f8>days of old!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>If a find like that doesn't make you an<f8>adventurer, I don't know what does!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 1<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 2<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 3<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 4<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 5<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 6<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 7<f8>adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Nicely done! You've just earned the rank of<f8>Master Adventurer!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It costs <e4> gil to participate.<f8>That all right?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It seems you're a little short on the gil.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Do you want to host a battle or join one?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Beg pardons, there seem not to be any takers.<f8>Let me return your <e4> gil.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It seems your battle was interrupted.<f8>I'll return your <e4> gil, then.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Hosting, then? Let's see if we can't find you<f8>an opponent.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Couldn't find an opponent for you, I'm afraid.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Excellent. I've found an opponent for you.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>All right then, choose the rules for your battle.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You won't be able to make changes later.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Very well, then. I'll inform your opponent of the<f8>rules. Just a moment, my lord.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Your opponent's choosing the melee rules.<f8>Won't be but a moment, my lord.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>All right, it seems your opponent has decided<f8>on the rules.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Now, select a location for the battle.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Are you sure this is where you want to fight?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I'll inform your opponent. Won't be but a<f8>moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Your opponent's selecting a location for the<f8>melee. Wait just a moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>All right, then. It seems your opponent has<f8>settled on a location.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Here are the missions I've got for you.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You want to take on a mission, eh?<f8>Mayhap I could show you the list?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You want to host a mission, or join one?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Hosting one, then? Wait just a moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Joining one, then? Wait just a moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Beg pardons, my lord. I couldn't find a partner<f8>for you.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Very well, then. I'll inform your partner.<f8>Wait just a moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Your partner's choosing a mission.<f8>Wait just a moment.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Seems even chocobos have fallen on hard times.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I've heard tell that spirits of the deceased oft<f8>appear in those parts.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It seems something of great import was<f8>lost.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Who wouldn't want to try their hand against<f8>the strongest chocobo in the world?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Count Thanes is famed for his riches.<f8>You'll get a fine reward for this, I wager.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>As I hear it, there are three kinds of bombs.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You'd best catch what comes your way,<f8>and throw it straight back!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Spirits attached to the world of the living...<f8>I wonder about their disposition.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>So, you want to test your skills?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You're the spitting images of those fugitives!<f8>Surely it's not but a mere semblance...
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Those spirits aren't to be trifled with. I've<f8>heard tell that magick has no effect on them.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>It seems the lad dreams of being attacked by<f8>demons. Truth be told, I'd wet the bed myself.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I've a bad feeling about this one.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I hear you succeeded in protecting those<f8>chocobos. That's a relief.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>The members of the caravan send you<f8>their gratitude.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Across the land, hunters speak your name!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I hear you succeeded in retrieving the treasure.<f8>Excellent work.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Did you enjoy the fete?<f8>Hm? ...You've had enough!?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Now business will thrive, and the economy<f8>is certain to recover!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I see... So the spirits were acquaintances<f8>of yours, were they?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>So you're now a Knight Templar, eh?<f8>What? You're not joining them!?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Thank the gods. This clears your name, aye?
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I'd wager you're the only ones who could've<f8>bested those things!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>I'm sure that's a load off the lad's mind.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>Never have you shone as brightly as you<f8>do now!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>You need to try a little harder, my lord.<f8>You can't disappoint people like that.
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 1<f8><eb>!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 2<f8><eb>!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 3<f8><eb>!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 4<f8><eb>!
<ec><75><SPEAKER>Tavernmaster<f8><e3>From this day forth, you're a level 5<f8><eb>!

<fa>Melee battles allow you to fight another player using Ad<f8>Hoc Mode. The only unit attribute that that will change<f8>permanently as a result of these battles is JP. Any items<f8>or units lost will be restored at the battle's end.

<fa>Rendezvousing with another player allows you to team up<f8>in order to complete cooperative missions. You may keep<f8>any items you obtain during battle in this mode, including<f8>any items that you steal or catch. Any items that you lose<f8>during battle will be restored at the battle's end. Should<f8>any of your equipment be stolen or rent in the course of<f8>combat, it will also be restored afterwards.<f8><f8><fa>Arithmeticks may not be used in cooperative missions,<f8>and using the Poach ability on monsters will not produce<f8>any items. Any units whose KO countdowns expire will be<f8>removed from the battle for its remaining duration. They<f8>are not lost, however, and will be restored to your party<f8>at the battle's end.<f8><f8><f8><fa>Enemy units who are charmed by means of the Entice<f8>ability will temporarily fight for you, but will not become<f8>permanent members of your party.<f8><f8><f8><f8><f8><f8><fa>Upon finishing a mission, you will be evaluated on a<f8>scale of one to five stars. Defeating more enemies or<f8>using a smaller number of units will earn you a higher<f8>evaluation and greater rewards. You may repeat a<f8>particular mission any number of times, allowing you to<f8>continue trying for a better evaluation.
